pi_items.source: FCC official website (https://broadbandmap.fcc.gov/) confirmed as live and official National Broadband Map

pi_items.verified_value: Comcast (Xfinity brand), Lumen (CenturyLink rebranded, Quantum Fiber brand), Astound (formerly Wave), plus Google Fiber
pi_items.source: Multiple web searches on Seattle broadband providers confirmed these major ISPs serve the downtown Seattle area. Note: Xfinity=Comcast brand, CenturyLink=Lumen (rebranded), Wave=Astound (acquired)

Lumen Quantum Fiber offers plans starting at 100 Mbps up to 500+ Mbps and multi-gig options. Comcast offers cable speeds up to 1,800-2,000 Mbps. 100 Mbps is a reasonable baseline for modern wired service.

pi_items.verified_value: Google Fiber, AT&T Fiber, Astound (formerly Grande), Spectrum (Charter brand), plus others
pi_items.source: Web searches confirmed all listed providers serve Austin. Google Fiber offers up to 8 Gbps, AT&T Fiber offers 100+ Mbps plans. Note: Spectrum=Charter brand, Grande=Astound (acquired)
AT&T Fiber plans start at 100 Mbps minimum (300 Mbps plan at $55/mo shown as baseline). Google Fiber's lowest tier is 1 Gbps. 100 Mbps is appropriate minimum for wired service.

pi_items.verified_value: Fidium (Consolidated Communications subsidiary), Consolidated Communications, Spectrum (Charter brand), FirstLight (Consolidated subsidiary)
pi_items.source: Web searches confirmed Fidium, Spectrum, and Consolidated/FirstLight all serve Portland Maine. Note: Fidium is owned by Consolidated, FirstLight is part of Consolidated, Spectrum=Charter brand
Spectrum offers plans starting at 100+ Mbps minimum in Portland. Consolidated/Fidium offers gigabit fiber plans. 50 Mbps is a conservative but realistic minimum for wired broadband availability.

pi_items.verified_value: 1000 4th Ave, Seattle, WA 98104 (Seattle Central Library - downtown location)
pi_items.source: Task description confirms this is Seattle Central Library address. Downtown Seattle has 97.2% Xfinity/Comcast cable coverage.

pi_items.metadata_value: 710 W Cesar Chavez St, Austin, TX 78701
pi_items.verified_value: 710 W Cesar Chavez St, Austin, TX 78701 (Austin Central Library - confirmed by Yelp and city listings)
pi_items.source: Multiple search results (Yelp, City of Austin, location services) confirm this is the correct address for Austin Central Library

pi_items.verified_value: 5 Monument Sq, Portland, ME 04101 (Portland Public Library - confirmed in multiple sources)
pi_items.source: Yelp, public library directories, and location services confirm this is the correct address for Portland Public Library

privileged_info_summary: The task references the real FCC National Broadband Map (broadbandmap.fcc.gov). All three library addresses are verified: Seattle Central Library at 1000 4th Ave, Austin Central Library at 710 W Cesar Chavez St, and Portland Public Library at 5 Monument Sq. The metadata provider lists are accurate for each city with verified major ISPs: Seattle has Comcast, Lumen, and Astound; Austin has Google Fiber, AT&T Fiber, and Astound; Portland has Fidium, Consolidated Communications, and Spectrum. Minimum download speed claims are realistic and verifiable: 100 Mbps for Seattle and Austin reflect actual service tiers from major providers, and 50 Mbps for Portland is a conservative minimum given Spectrum and Consolidated's service offerings.
